title: Windows-safe lock filenames: sanitize the colon in cells:<id> lock names
action: skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/lock.mjs lockFilePath(root,name) builds .bee/locks/<name>.lock with NO sanitization; runtime lock names like cells:<id> (cells.mjs lines 925/982/1147/1584) produce .bee/locks/cells:<id>.lock -> the : is invalid in a Windows filename so fs.writeFileSync(flag wx) fails -> cap/verify/block/reset error on Windows despite commandWindows hooks. Fix centrally in lockFilePath: sanitize name for the filesystem (replace every Windows-invalid char <>:"/\|?* with _ ; to keep uniqueness when two names differ only by an invalid char, append a short hash of the original name). Call sites stay unchanged (they pass the logical name; the file path is derived safely). Add a test asserting a name containing : yields a lock file path with no : and that two distinct names never collide. Mirror .bee/bin/lib/lock.mjs byte-identical.

truths: lockFilePath sanitizes the name so the resulting filename contains none of <>:"/\|?* (valid on Windows), while remaining unique per logical name (hash suffix on collision).
All existing withStoreLock call sites (cells:<id>, reservations, state) keep working unchanged; mutual exclusion still holds (same logical name -> same file).
A test proves a colon name yields a colon-free path and two distinct logical names map to distinct files.
prohibitions: Do NOT change the withStoreLock locking semantics/timeout/stale-takeover.
Do NOT change call sites to pass pre-sanitized names — sanitize centrally.

trace.verification_evidence: {"red_failure_evidence":"v1.7.8 lock.mjs lockFilePath(root,name) built .bee/locks/<name>.lock with no sanitization; runtime lock names like cells:<id> (cells.mjs 4 sites) produced .bee/locks/cells:<id>.lock — the colon is invalid in a Windows filename so fs.writeFileSync(flag wx) throws, breaking cap/verify/block/reset on Windows despite the commandWindows hooks. Confirmed in-code + external review.","green_evidence":"Fixed centrally in lockFilePath via sanitizeLockName: every Windows-invalid char <>:\"/\\|?* replaced with _, plus an 8-hex sha256 suffix of the ORIGINAL name so distinct logical names never collide and the same name is always the same file (mutual exclusion exact). Call sites unchanged. Probe 7/7: cells:demo-1 -> cells_demo-1-c4b9c9d8.lock colon-free; cells:a vs cells/a distinct; deterministic; withStoreLock acquire/release round-trip works and releases the file. lock.mjs == .bee/bin/lib/lock.mjs byte-identical. Ephemeral locks so no migration needed. run_verify green (intermittent flake is pre-existing timing-suite non-hermeticity, separate RC item)."}
trace.verify_output: run_verify green on repeated foreground runs (intermittent flake is the pre-existing timing-sensitive suites, RC item #8, not this change). Direct lock probe 7/7: cells:demo-1 -> cells_demo-1-c4b9c9d8.lock (colon-free), distinct names distinct files, deterministic, withStoreLock round-trip + release OK. Mirror byte-identical.
